steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a restrictive vs. non-restrictive clause question. The noun phrase “one European city” needs a clause that identifies which city — the one many travelers recommend. The decisive clue is the word “one”: it signals that the clause must restrict the noun, which means no comma and the relative pronoun “that.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘city, that’: “one European city, that many travelers recommend” — a comma before “that” in a restrictive clause violates standard usage; restrictive “that” never takes a preceding comma. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘city, which’: “one European city, which many travelers recommend” — a comma + “which” signals a non-restrictive clause that adds extra information rather than identifying the noun; the clause here is identifying, not parenthetical. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘city in which,’: “one European city in which, many travelers recommend” — “recommend in which” is ungrammatical; you recommend a city, not recommend in a city, so “in which” is the wrong preposition-relative construction here. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘city that’: “one European city that many travelers recommend” — restrictive “that” with no comma correctly identifies which city is meant. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
